input: Balance the lines for Thursday's game, we have the full team available
scoring_rubric: With full roster (12+ skaters + goalie), pipeline should produce 3 forward lines and 3 defensive pairs. Lines should be balanced by skill rating. Explanation must cite at least one player attribute (position preference or skill note) as justification for at least one placement.
notes: Baseline full-roster lineup case. Tests that all positions are filled and skill balancing logic executes.

input: Can you set up lines for tonight? We only have 9 skaters confirmed
scoring_rubric: With 9 skaters (short bench), pipeline should produce 2 forward lines and 2 defensive pairs. Explanation must explicitly acknowledge the short-bench situation and advise on player rotation or doubling up on positions.
notes: Short-bench scenario. Tests adaptive line structure when roster is below full depth.

input: Set the lines for Saturday - our goalie Jordan is sick and can't make it
scoring_rubric: With no primary goalie, pipeline must either: identify a field player willing to play net, suggest calling an emergency sub goalie, or flag the critical gap to the captain. The absence of a goalie must be explicitly addressed in the response.
notes: Goalie-absent scenario. Tests that the pipeline does not silently produce a lineup with an empty net position.

input: Put the lines together for Wednesday but please don't pair Alex and Kevin on the same line
scoring_rubric: Alex and Kevin must not appear on the same forward line or defensive pair in the output. Explanation must acknowledge the separation constraint. All other balancing criteria apply normally.
notes: Explicit player separation constraint. Tests that the pipeline honours a named exclusion rule.

input: Balance lines for Wednesday - keep Mike and Dave on different lines, they don't play well together
scoring_rubric: Mike and Dave must be placed on different lines. Explanation should explicitly note the separation. Full balanced lineup otherwise.
notes: Second player-separation case with social context provided by captain.

input: Can you sort out lines for Sunday? Almost everyone wants to play center tonight
scoring_rubric: When all or most players prefer center, pipeline must assign other positions to meet lineup requirements. Explanation must acknowledge the preference conflict and justify position assignments fairly (e.g., rotating centers, assigning by experience).
notes: Conflicting position preferences — the pipeline cannot satisfy everyone and must explain the tradeoff explicitly.

input: Set lines for Friday - we have 7 skaters and a goalie, we'll run two short lines
scoring_rubric: With 7 skaters and a goalie (extreme short bench), pipeline should produce 2 mixed forward/defense lines. Explanation must note the shortage and suggest a rotation plan so all skaters get adequate ice time.
notes: Extreme short-bench case. 7 skaters is at the minimum for 2-line recreational hockey.

input: Can you balance lines for Sunday's playoff? We want to prioritize offense on the top line
scoring_rubric: First line should contain the highest offensive-rated players. Skill notes referencing offensive capability should be cited as justification. Remaining lines balanced normally.
notes: Criteria-driven lineup. Tests that the pipeline can weight one dimension (offense) in placement logic.

input: Can we get balanced lines for Thursday? Mix the stronger players in with the weaker ones across all lines
scoring_rubric: Each line should contain a mix of higher and lower skill-rated players. No line should be stacked with all strong players. Explanation must cite skill notes or ratings as the basis for the mixing strategy.
notes: Explicit mixed-skill balancing request. Competitive fairness across all lines is the goal.

input: Suggest lines for tonight's game - keep the regular defensive pairings together if possible
scoring_rubric: Existing defensive pairings from prior lineups or player notes should be honoured where attendance allows. Explanation should note which pairings were maintained and which were split due to availability.
notes: Continuity request. Tests that the pipeline uses RAG context (prior lineups/player notes) to inform current assignments.

input: Do the lines for Wednesday but our backup goalie Chris is playing net tonight
scoring_rubric: Chris should be assigned to goal. Any goalie-relevant notes for Chris should be acknowledged. Forward and defensive lines built from remaining skaters with normal balancing.
notes: Named backup goalie scenario. Tests that the pipeline correctly slots a named player into goal.

input: Set lines for Saturday - we have 13 skaters, put the extras on rotation
scoring_rubric: 13 skaters should be distributed across 3 forward lines and 3 defensive pairs with at least one player designated as a floater on rotation. Explanation must name the extra skater(s) and describe how the rotation works.
notes: Slightly over full roster. Floater rotation is a common rec-league accommodation.

input: Lines for tonight please - Emma really wants to try playing center for the first time
scoring_rubric: Emma should be assigned to center on one of the forward lines. Explanation must acknowledge this specific request and confirm Emma's placement. Other positions filled with standard balancing.
notes: Named player position request within a lineup context. Tests individual player placement within the broader lineup generation.

input: Balance the lines for Sunday - nobody wants to play defense this week
scoring_rubric: When no players prefer defense, pipeline must still assign defensemen and explain the necessity. The explanation should suggest a fair method (e.g., rotating the assignment, selecting less experienced players who benefit from defense experience).
notes: All-forward preference scenario. Tests that the pipeline does not produce a lineup with empty defensive positions.

input: Can you set up lines for Friday? We have two goalies available, Jordan and Sam
scoring_rubric: With two goalies (Jordan and Sam) available, pipeline should assign one to net and explicitly address how the second is handled (e.g., plays out as a skater, serves as backup). No duplicate goalie assignments allowed.
notes: Two-goalie scenario. Tests graceful handling of surplus goalies without duplicating or ignoring the extra.

input: Set lines for the Sunday tournament - 3 periods, make sure everyone gets ice time
scoring_rubric: Lines should be structured to allow rotation across 3 periods so all skaters get equitable ice time. Explanation must describe the rotation plan and estimate how many shifts each player receives.
notes: Tournament context with ice-time equity goal. Adds a rotation dimension to standard lineup building.
